perturbation.original_text: Subject to the doctrine of sequential liability, payment shall be made to the appropriate Party net 30 days from the end of the calendar month.
perturbation.changed_text: Payment shall be made to the appropriate Party net 30 days from the end of the calendar month, regardless of any existing liens or encumbrances on the received funds.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters payment terms by disregarding potential legal claims like liens, contradicting standard commercial laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Uniform Commercial Code - Secured Transactions - Priority of Security Interests
perturbation.law_explanation: UCC § 9-322 establishes the priority of security interests. This modification attempts to bypass established priority rules by mandating payment irrespective of liens, creating a direct contradiction with secured transaction law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 9-322. PRIORITIES AMONG CONFLICTING SECURITY INTERESTS IN AND AGRICULTURAL LIENS ON SAME COLLATERAL. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 9-322. PRIORITIES AMONG CONFLICTING SECURITY INTERESTS IN AND AGRICULTURAL LIENS ON SAME COLLATERAL. (a) [General priority rules.] Except as otherwise provided in this section, priority among conflicting security interests and agricultural liens in the same collateral is determined according to the following rules: (1) Conflicting perfected security interests and agricultural liens rank according to priority in time of filing or perfection. Priority dates from the earlier of the time a filing covering the collateral is first made or the security interest or agricultural lien is first perfected, if there is no period thereafter when there is neither filing nor perfection. (2) A perfected security interest or agricultural lien has priority over a conflicting unperfected security interest or agricultural lien. (3) The first security interest or agricultural lien to attach or become effective has priority if conflicting security interests and agricultural liens are unperfected. (b) [Time of perfection: proceeds and supporting obligations.] For the purposes of subsection (a)(1): (1) the time of filing or perfection as to a security interest in collateral is also the time of filing or perfection as to a security interest in proceeds ; and (2) the time of filing or perfection as to a security interest in collateral supported by a supporting obligation is also the time of filing or perfection as to a security interest in the supporting obligation. (c) [Special priority rules: proceeds and supporting obligations.] Except as otherwise provided in subsection (f), a security interest in collateral which qualifies for priority over a conflicting security interest under Section 9-327 , 9-328 , 9-329 , 9-330 , or 9-331 also has priority over a conflicting security int
perturbation.justification: The modified text mandates payment 'regardless of any existing liens or encumbrances,' which directly contradicts UCC § 9-322. This law establishes the priority of security interests, stating that conflicting perfected security interests rank according to priority in time of filing or perfection. By requiring payment irrespective of liens, the modified text attempts to bypass these established priority rules, creating a direct conflict with secured transaction law as explained by the LLM.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient and compensation to Kubient under the applicable exhibit and/or schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and perpetually thereafter, Customer agrees not to work with, directly or indirectly, any Third-Party that Customer comes to know through disclosure by Kubient as part of the Services, without the express written consent of Kubient and compensation to Kubient under the applicable exhibit and/or schedule.
perturbation.explanation: This change extends the non-circumvention clause in perpetuity, which is generally unenforceable and an unreasonable restraint of trade.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600 generally invalidates contracts that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. Extending the non-circumvention agreement in perpetuity is an unreasonable restraint and likely unenforceable under this law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, BPC 16600 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Business and Professions Code - BPC DIVISION 7. GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the non-circumvention agreement 'perpetually thereafter,' which contradicts California Business and Professions Code Section 16600. The law snippet states that 'every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void.' By extending the restriction indefinitely, the changed text creates an unreasonable restraint of trade, making it likely unenforceable under California law. The original text limited the restriction to one year, which is more likely to be considered reasonable. The changed text now creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of one (1) year following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer shall not, without Kubient's prior written consent, directly or indirectly (i) solicit or encourage any person to leave the employment or other service of Kubient; or (ii) hire, on behalf of Customer or any other person or entity, any person who has left the employment of Kubient within the one (1) year period following the termination or end of that person's employment.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term and any renewal terms of the Agreement, and for a period of ten (10) years following the expiration or earlier termination thereof, Customer shall not, without Kubient's prior written consent, directly or indirectly (i) solicit or encourage any person to leave the employment or other service of Kubient; or (ii) hire, on behalf of Customer or any other person or entity, any person who has left the employment of Kubient within the one (1) year period following the termination or end of that person's employment.
perturbation.explanation: Extending the non-solicitation period to ten years is an unreasonable restraint on trade and may be deemed unenforceable. It significantly restricts the former employee's ability to seek employment.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600
perturbation.law_explanation: California Business and Professions Code Section 16600 generally invalidates contracts that restrain a person from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business. Extending the non-solicitation agreement to ten years presents an unreasonable restraint and is likely unenforceable under this law.

GENERAL BUSINESS REGULATIONS [16000 - 18107] ( Division 7 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 61. ) PART 2. PRESERVATION AND REGULATION OF COMPETITION [16600 - 17365] ( Part 2 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) CHAPTER 1. Contracts in Restraint of Trade [16600 - 16607] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1941, Ch. 526. ) 16600. (a) Except as provided in this chapter, every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void. (b) (1) This section shall be read broadly, in accordance with Edwards v. Arthur Andersen LLP (2008) 44 Cal.4th 937, to void the application of any noncompete agreement in an employment context, or any noncompete clause in an employment contract, no matter how narrowly tailored, that does not satisfy an exception in this chapter. (2) This subdivision does not constitute a change in, but is declaratory of, existing law. (c) This section shall not be limited to contracts where the person being restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business is a party to the contract. (Amended by Stats. 2023, Ch. 828, Sec. 1. (AB 1076) Effective January 1, 2024.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the non-solicitation period to ten years, which creates a legal contradiction. The original text had a one-year restriction, while the modification extends it to ten years. According to the provided 'Law Explanation', this change introduces an unreasonable restraint and is likely unenforceable. This is supported by 'Scraped Law Snippet 1', specifically California Business and Professions Code Section 16600(a), which states that 'every contract by which anyone is restrained from engaging in a lawful profession, trade, or business of any kind is to that extent void.' The extended restriction period directly contradicts this law by placing an unreasonable burden on former employees seeking new employment, and thus is a 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction'.
